meta_title: For Students: email automation to Solve no portfolio (Beginner Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to email automation and overcome no portfolio. A Beginner-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Email Automation to Solve No Portfolio (Checklist)
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with no portfolio. Learn to build automated email systems using Antigravity and Claude Code in this high-impact beginner checklist.
content.answer_block: Building a high-utility email automation engine solves your 'no portfolio' problem by demonstrating real-world engineering capability. By integrating Antigravity for rapid backend deployment and Claude Code for iterative development, you create a scalable, production-ready tool that proves you can solve complex business problems, not just follow tutorials.
content.intro:
The blank screen of a portfolio is the silent killer of junior careers. While your peers chase generic CRUD apps, you are about to build a functional automation engine that solves a genuine business pain. This isn't just code; it's proof of your ability to bridge the gap between technical complexity and real-world utility.

We are moving past theory. By leveraging Antigravity for infrastructure and Claude Code for intelligent iteration, you will transform a simple script into a professional-grade automation tool. This project is your ticket to standing out in a crowded market by showing recruiters you can build, deploy, and maintain systems that actually work.

steps.title: Define the Automation Logic
steps.description: Establish the trigger-action flow for your email system. Focus on a specific use case like 'automated follow-up for lead magnets' to make the project tangible.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean architecture for an email automation service that triggers on a webhook event, processes user data, and queues a personalized email via SendGrid.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize project structure for email automation backend using Node.js and TypeScript'

steps.title: Configure the Infrastructure
steps.description: Use Antigravity to stand up your environment. This ensures your project is production-ready rather than just a local script.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a cloud-native configuration for an email worker service that includes environment variable management and secure API key storage.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Setup environment configuration and integrate SDKs for email provider'

steps.title: Implement Intelligent Templates
steps.description: Use AI to generate dynamic email content. This adds a layer of sophistication that standard static templates lack.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a template engine function that takes user JSON data and renders a personalized email body using Handlebars or EJS.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement dynamic template rendering logic with error handling for missing fields'

steps.title: Build the Webhook Listener
steps.description: Create the gateway that allows your automation to receive data from external sources like Typeform or your own landing page.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a secure Express.js endpoint to receive webhook payloads, validate the signature, and trigger the email worker.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create secure webhook endpoint with validation middleware'

steps.title: Deploy and Monitor
steps.description: Move your code to a live environment. Monitoring is what separates a student project from a professional application.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a deployment script and a simple logging strategy to track email delivery success and failure rates.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Finalize deployment scripts and add basic logging for monitoring'

pro_tips: Always implement exponential backoff in your email retry logic to handle rate limits gracefully.
Store your email logs in a structured database rather than just console logs for easier debugging.
Use environment-specific configuration files to prevent leaking your API keys to production.
Add a 'test mode' flag to your code to prevent sending actual emails during development cycles.
Document your API endpoints with Swagger or OpenAPI to show senior-level project management skills.
content.next_steps: Once deployed, add a README to your GitHub repository that explains the business problem you solved, the architecture, and a link to a live demo.
content.conversion_block: You now have the exact blueprint to transform your technical skills into a career-defining project. Stop waiting for permission to be an engineer. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
